perturbation.original_text: "Territory" means the geographical region described in the attached EXHIBIT D.
perturbation.changed_text: "Territory" means the geographical region described in the attached EXHIBIT D, but shall not include any regions where TouchStar already has established reseller relationships.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to the definition of 'Territory' introduces an in-text contradiction. Initially, 'Territory' is unambiguously defined as the area described in EXHIBIT D. The added clause, 'but shall not include any regions where TouchStar already has established reseller relationships,' creates ambiguity. It's unclear whether existing reseller relationships override EXHIBIT D, creating uncertainty about the reseller's exclusive rights. This new definition can be leveraged by TouchStar to limit the Reseller's operations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 NONEXCLUSIVE RESELLER. Subject to applicable Legal Requirements: (a) TouchStar hereby appoints Reseller as its nonexclusive value- added reseller for the limited purposes of promoting, marketing, distributing, licensing and selling the TouchStar Software and Support Services in the Territory, and Reseller accepts the appointment as such. Reseller shall have the right under this Agreement to promote, market, distribute, license and sell the TouchStar Software and Support Services to Customers in the Territory. TouchStar reserves the right to provide the TouchStar Software and Support Services to other customers in the Territory and/or to appoint additional distributors or representatives in all or any part of the Territory.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Territory' relied solely on EXHIBIT D. The change adds an exception for areas with existing reseller relationships. This directly contradicts section 2.1, which grants the reseller non-exclusive rights 'in the Territory'. The new exception creates uncertainty about the scope of those rights, as EXHIBIT D may now be overridden by TouchStar's pre-existing relationships. This introduces a strong in-text contradiction as to the actual territory the Reseller has rights to.

perturbation.original_text: Reseller shall not import, promote, distribute, license, market or sell any products in 6 the Territory which directly compete with the TouchStar Software or Support Services.
perturbation.changed_text: Reseller shall not import, promote, distribute, license, market or sell any products in the Territory which directly compete with the TouchStar Software or Support Services, except for products pre-approved by TouchStar, which approval shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'except for products pre-approved by TouchStar, which approval shall not be unreasonably withheld' creates an in-text contradiction. The original text strictly prohibits the Reseller from selling competing products. The added exception undermines this restriction. What constitutes 'unreasonably withheld' is subjective and open to interpretation, potentially leading to disputes and making the non-compete clause unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2 TERMINATION AT TOUCHSTAR OPTION. TouchStar may terminate this Agreement upon the occurrence and continuation of any of the following events, with the understanding 17 that, if no cure period specifically is stated with regard to an event, then no cure period for such event applies: (a) Reseller fails to make any payment of Fees due to TouchStar under this Agreement and such failure remains unremedied for a period of ten (10) days; (b) Reseller breaches any of its other obligations under this Agreement and such breach remains unremedied for a period of thirty (30) days; (c) Reseller or any Other Reseller takes any action to Reverse Engineer the TouchStar Software; (d) Reseller fails to comply with applicable Legal Requirements, including Government Controls; (e) Reseller fails to reach the Quotas established by the parties; (f) Reseller repeatedly breaches any of its obligations under this Agreement, even though Reseller remedies each such breach within the applicable time period specified above; (g) Reseller fails to execute an Other Reseller Agreement with any Other Reseller; (h) Reseller or any Other Reseller fails to deliver a License Agreement to a Customer; (i) Reseller fails to indemnify TouchStar, its Affiliates and its and their respective directors, officers, employees, agents and representatives for any claims related to or arising under any Other Reseller Agreement or the use by TouchStar of the Reseller Marks; (j) Reseller is negligent in the fulfillment of its obligations to market and resell the TouchStar Software; (k) Reseller breaches any of its obligations relating to the Intellectual Property Rights or Confidential Information; (l) Reseller, any of Reseller's officers, directors, or shareholders, or any entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with Reseller promotes, sells, or offers for sale any product or other item that is, in TouchStar's reasonable opinion, competitive with or capable of being substituted for any of the TouchStar Software; or Reseller engages in overt or subvert forms of boycott of the TouchStar Software, including the offer for sale of any product or other item that is, in TouchStar's reasonable opinion, competitive with or capable of being substituted for any of the TouchStar Software; (m) In the event of a sale, conveyance, transfer or other disposition, in any transaction or series of transactions that results, directly or indirectly, in a
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibits the reseller from selling any competing products. The changed text introduces an exception allowing the reseller to sell competing products with TouchStar's pre-approval. The contradicted text, specifically section (l), allows TouchStar to terminate the agreement if the reseller promotes, sells, or offers for sale any product that is, in TouchStar's reasonable opinion, competitive with the TouchStar software. The changed text introduces a contradiction because, under the original agreement, selling a competing product would be grounds for termination, but with the added exception, it's permissible if pre-approved, thus directly conflicting with TouchStar's termination rights as outlined in section (l).

perturbation.original_text: TouchStar may change the Fees from time to time in its sole discretion; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that new Fees shall not apply to any agreement to provide Support Services accepted by TouchStar before Reseller receives notice of the change.
perturbation.changed_text: TouchStar may change the Fees from time to time in its sole discretion; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that new Fees shall not apply to any agreement to provide Support Services accepted by TouchStar before Reseller receives notice of the change, unless the change is mandated by unforeseen economic circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'unless the change is mandated by unforeseen economic circumstances' creates uncertainty in the fee structure. While the original text provides a clear exception to fee changes, this clause introduces a vague and subjective condition. 'Unforeseen economic circumstances' is open to interpretation, allowing TouchStar to potentially circumvent the original fee agreement and unilaterally raise prices, which contradicts and undermines the original intent of the Fees structure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) TouchStar will invoice Reseller for payment of the Fee incurred within ten (10) days after the end of each billable month. Payment of the Fees shall be due and payable within ten (10) days of the date of the invoice. All or any portion of the Fees not paid when due shall bear interest at the rate of one and one-half (1.5%) per month, calculated from the date such payment is due until the date on which such payment is made, inclusive; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that if such interest rate exceeds the amount allowed by applicable law, then the interest rate shall be adjusted to reflect the maximum amount allowed by such applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The added clause "unless the change is mandated by unforeseen economic circumstances" in the Changed Text directly contradicts the expectation of fixed fees outlined in both the original text and the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text specifies payment terms based on an invoice for the 'Fee incurred', implying a predictable and stable fee structure. The addition creates a loophole that undermines the agreed-upon payment terms, allowing TouchStar to potentially alter fees retroactively due to vaguely defined economic circumstances. This introduces uncertainty and directly conflicts with the fixed-fee expectation implied in section (b) regarding invoicing and payment.
